title: ⚠ MAP KEY — these are MODERN political borders
description: All coloured regions are MODERN country borders + Mexican state borders. They do NOT represent pre-colonial Zapotec, Mixtec, Nahua, or Huichol territories — those Indigenous sovereignties pre-date modern borders by millennia.

Jalisco state (modern borders) — Tequila DO
description: Modern political border. The Tequila Denominación de Origen (1974) covers Jalisco + specific municipalities in Nayarit, Michoacán, Guanajuato, Tamaulipas. Only blue agave (Agave tequilana Weber var. azul) grown in DO zones can be called 'tequila'.

title: Oaxaca + mezcal DO states (modern borders)
description: Modern political borders. Mezcal DO (1994) covers Oaxaca + 8 other states: Durango, Guanajuato, Guerrero, Michoacán, Puebla, San Luis Potosí, Tamaulipas, Zacatecas. Zapotec + Mixtec indigenous producers maintain traditional methods.

title: Mexico (modern borders) — full national context
description: Modern political border (post-1917 Constitution). Mexico's full territory — DO regions are specific but cultural tradition of agave distillation spans many states and indigenous nations.

title: USA (modern borders) — world's #1 tequila market (75% of exports)
description: Modern political border. 75% of all Mexican tequila exports go to the US. California, Texas, and Florida lead consumption. Premium tequila is the fastest-growing spirits category in the US 2018–2024.
